name: Tribeca
tagline: The Quiet Luxury of the Triangle Below Canal
description: Once an industrial district, Tribeca has evolved into NYC's most sought-after neighborhood for those who value privacy, space, and architectural integrity. A masterclass in quiet luxury.

highlights.name: The Odeon
highlights.desc: The quintessential brasserie that defined the neighborhood.

highlights.name: Pier 26
highlights.desc: Lush gardens and tide decks with panoramic Hudson views.

highlights.name: Hook & Ladder 8
highlights.desc: The historic firehouse famously seen in Ghostbusters.

name: SOHO
description: The world's largest collection of cast-iron architecture. SOHO is an open-air museum where luxury fashion meets the ghosts of the 1970s avant-garde art scene.

highlights.name: Mercer Street
highlights.desc: The heart of the cast-iron district's grand facades.

highlights.name: Balthazar
highlights.desc: An iconic French bistro that never goes out of style.

name: DUMBO
description: Down Under the Manhattan Bridge Overpass. A waterfront district defined by its dramatic bridge framings, tech hubs, and impeccably restored factory warehouses.

highlights.name: Empire Stores
highlights.desc: A warehouse-turned-destination for dining and creative office space.

highlights.name: Jane's Carousel
highlights.desc: A meticulously restored 1922 carousel housed in a Jean Nouvel pavilion.

highlights.name: Washington Street
highlights.desc: The world-famous 'Manhattan Bridge in the frame' view.
